patch.value: Extension FlagDefinition lacks 'list' and 'default' fields — extension-registered flags cannot match core comma-list contract

The FlagDefinition interface in extension-types.ts (line 398) has: long, short, value_name, description, required, enabled, visible, type/value_type, plus an index signature `[key: string]: unknown`. The core CliFlagContract in cli-contracts.ts has an additional `list?: boolean` field (used by coalesceRepeatedListFlags) and there is no `default` field. An extension that registers a comma-list flag via registerFlags cannot declare list:true to get the same accumulation behavior as core flags (e.g. `--tags`). This means multi-value extension flags lose values on repeated invocations. The `[key: string]: unknown` index signature silently accepts list:true but it is not documented, typed, or enforced.

- FlagDefinition gains typed `list?: boolean` and `default?` fields
- Extension multi-value flags accumulate via coalesceRepeatedListFlags like core `--tags`
- Typed (not via index signature); documented; covered by a test

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Audit 2026-06-07 (SDK agent F1): root cause is deeper than missing list/default fields — extension-registered flags bypass the bootstrap argv normalizer ENTIRELY. resolveSubcommandFlagContractsForCommand (sdk/cli-contracts.ts) is a hard-coded switch over CORE commands only and never consults ExtensionRegistrationRegistry.flags, so extension flags get no alias/typo/comma-list normalization (parsed via the separate loose-option path in cli/extension-command-options.ts). Fix must add list/default to FlagDefinition AND merge active extension flag registrations into the normalizer lookup.
